Farn Pourrcal PnecrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
December 31,2004
Jon Waldie, Chief Administrative Offi cer
California State Assernbly
Post Office 8ox942849
Sacramento, CA 94249
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. l-04-257
Dear Mr. Waldie:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the California Assernbly
Committee on Rules for advice regarding the gift provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the'Act').r Your question asks i?the udri""-prorrided to the Senate Rules Committee,
regarding free airport parking and shuttle services provided by the City of Los Angeles,
as set forth in the Blessing Advice Letter, No. A-01-068, is also applicable to the
Assembly Committee on Rules.2
In Blessing) supra, we advised that
"[a]s long as the City of Los Angeles does not speciff which
legislators should receive the passes, the Senate Rules Committee
accepts the passes under Regulation 19944.2, and the city is not
providing the passes as part of its eflorts to lobby the legislature,
then the city is not required to report the passes ... nor would the
legislators who use the passes be required to report thern as gfts.',
Regulation 189M.2 provides an exception to the general gift reporting rules when
a gift is made to an official's agency. In order for this exception to apply, the regulation
provides four criteria that must be satisfied:
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - g1)l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18^997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
In Blessing,the requestor rvut G City of Los Angeles, and our response, although providing
information concerning the reporting obligations of the recipient of the gift, wis addressed at tle reportLg
obligations of a lobbyist employerprovider of the gift. Although the advice provided herein is the iame as
provided inBlessing, we are limiting our advice to your specific question dealing with the reporting
obligations of the recipient of the gift and do not address the corresponding reporting obligations of the
provider of the gift. (Ibid.)
d;1a


File No. I-04-257
Page No. 2
"(1). The agency receives and controls the payment.
Q). Thepayment is used for official agencybusiness.
(3). The agency, in its sole discretion, determines the specific
official or officials who shall use the payment....
(a). The agency memorializes the payment in a written public
record which embodies the requirements of subdivisions (a)(1) to
(aX3) of this regulation..." (Regulation 18944.2.)
Accordingly, as we stated in Blessing, supra, that:
"[a]though it appears that the parking and shuttle passes do qualifu
as a gift reportable by the lobbyist employer under the activity
expense category, if the Senate Rules Committee [here the
Assembly Committee on Rules] takes whatever steps are necessary
to satisfu the standards established in Regulationlgg44.2,the
passes will be considered gifts to the Senate Rules Committee
[here the Assembly Committee on Rules] and not to the officials
who incidentally use them." (See also Kelly Advice Letter,No. A-
89-458, copy enclosed, and In re Stone (1977) 3 FppC Ops. 52.)
In other words, as long as the Assembly committee on Rules meets the
requiranents provided in regulation 18944.2, and the free airport parking and shuttle
services are provided to the committee on that basis, the employees of the committee who
use the passes would not be required to rqrort them as gifts.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
By:
Lenkeit
Division
Enclosures - Reg. 18329
Kelly Advice Letter No. ,4.-89-458

sections.question: Your question asks if the advice provided to the Senate Rules Committee, regarding free airport parking and shuttle services provided by the City of Los Angeles, as set forth in the Blessing Advice Letter, No. A-01-068, is also applicable to the Assembly Committee on Rules.
sections.conclusion: In other words, as long as the Assembly committee on Rules meets the requirements provided in regulation 18944.2, and the free airport parking and shuttle services are provided to the committee on that basis, the employees of the committee who use the passes would not be required to report them as gifts.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the FPPC's prior advice regarding gift reporting exemptions for free airport parking and shuttle services provided by the City of Los Angeles applies equally to the California Assembly Committee on Rules as it did to the Senate Rules Committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Assembly Committee on Rules may rely on the same gift reporting exemption as the Senate Rules Committee, provided the Committee satisfies all four criteria under Regulation 18944.2, including controlling the passes, using them for official business, determining which officials use them in its sole discretion, and memorializing the arrangement in a written public record.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter responding to specific request. Clear question and conclusion sections identified. References prior Blessing Advice Letter (A-01-068) and related regulations. FPPC counsel Luisa Menchaca signed response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your question asks if the advice provided to the Senate Rules Committee, regarding free airport parking and shuttle services provided by the City of Los Angeles, as set forth in the Blessing Advice Letter, No. A-01-068, is also applicable to the Assembly Committee on Rules.

CONCLUSION: In other words, as long as the Assembly committee on Rules meets the requirements provided in regulation 18944.2, and the free airport parking and shuttle services are provided to the committee on that basis, the employees of the committee who use the passes would not be required to report them as gifts.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the California Assembly Committee on Rules may apply the same gift reporting exemption for free airport parking and shuttle services from the City of Los Angeles that was previously approved for the Senate Rules Committee, contingent upon compliance with Regulation 18944.2 requirements.
